food_name: Make Your Own Khao (Customisable Rice Bowl)

local_names.thai: ข้าวตามใจชอบ

aliases: ข้าวตามใจชอบ

thai: ข้าวตามใจชอบ

food_subgroup: Customisable rice bowl with vegetables, protein, and grains

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for a customisable Thai rice bowl. FOODON_00002809 (rice dish) is the closest defensible parent category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A000E (composite rice-based dish) is the closest parent. No specific code exists for a build-your-own Thai rice bowl format.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code exists for a customisable Thai rice bowl dish. SNOMED CT does not cover restaurant-format composite dishes at this specificity.

main_ingredients: choice of protein (chicken, tofu, egg, or seafood)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 400 g, representing a full customisable rice bowl with a jasmine rice base, mixed vegetables, a chosen protein, and sauce, as typically served at Khao - Funan in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Thai Jasmine Rice Cooked with Mixed Vegetables and Lean Protein — composite estimate based on USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for cooked jasmine rice, mixed vegetables, and grilled chicken breast
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central SR Legacy
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry exists for a customisable Thai rice bowl in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, or FAO/INFOODS SEA databases. Values are estimated as a weighted composite of USDA SR Legacy entries: cooked jasmine rice (FDC ID 169756, ~55% by weight), mixed cooked vegetables (FDC ID 169230, ~25% by weight), and grilled chicken breast without skin (FDC ID 171477, ~20% by weight). This represents a typical mid-range bowl configuration. Actual nutrient content will vary substantially depending on customer choices of protein, sauce, and toppings.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2021 — white rice (jasmine) cooked
gi_source.source_match_notes: No GI value exists for a customisable Thai rice bowl as a composite dish. GI of 56 is derived from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for cooked jasmine white rice (GI ~64–68 range), moderated downward to approximately 56 to account for the diluting effect of mixed vegetables (low GI, ~15) and lean protein (negligible GI contribution), weighted by approximate proportions in the bowl (55% rice, 25% vegetables, 20% protein). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (56 × 74.0) / 100 = 41.4, where carbohydrate per 400 g serving = 18.5 × 4 = 74.0 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content from sauces and seasoning
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should request low-sodium sauce options and avoid high-salt condiments such as fish sauce or soy-based dressings, as the estimated sodium content of approximately 210 mg per 100 g (840 mg per full bowl) can contribute meaningfully to the daily sodium limit of 2000 mg recommended by the Health Promotion Board Singapore.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium glycemic index from jasmine rice base and moderate glycemic load per serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should be aware that the jasmine rice base contributes a medium GI of approximately 56 and a glycemic load of approximately 41 per full bowl; choosing a smaller rice portion, increasing vegetable volume, and selecting a lean protein without sugary sauces can meaningfully reduce postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat content with predominantly lean protein and vegetable components
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The estimated saturated fat content of approximately 0.4 g per 100 g is low, and the bowl's emphasis on vegetables and lean protein supports a heart-healthy dietary pattern; however, individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid high-fat protein options such as pork belly or fried items and request oil-free or light-sauce preparations.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Make Your Own Khao is a customisable Thai-inspired rice bowl concept served at Khao - Funan in Singapore, priced at SGD 18.80. The word 'Khao' (ข้าว) means 'rice' in Thai, and the dish centres on a jasmine rice base that customers personalise with a selection of toppings including mixed vegetables, fresh herbs, and a choice of protein such as grilled chicken, tofu, seafood, or egg, finished with a sauce of the customer's choosing. The bowl is assembled to order and is classified as minimally processed, with the rice typically steamed and the protein grilled or lightly stir-fried. The flavour profile is mild to savoury with aromatic herbal notes, and the texture contrasts soft rice with crisp or lightly cooked vegetables. Nutritionally, a standard 400 g bowl provides approximately 472 kcal, 28.8 g protein, 7.2 g fat, and 74 g carbohydrate, making it a moderate-calorie, protein-adequate meal. The dish is positioned in the healthy eating segment and is popular among health-conscious diners in Singapore's central business district. Clinically, the jasmine rice base carries a medium glycemic index of approximately 56, and the glycemic load per full bowl is approximately 41, which is relevant for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes. Sodium content is moderate and depends heavily on sauce selection. The dish is generally dairy-free and can be adapted to vegetarian or vegan preferences depending on protein and sauce choices.

jasmine rice: ข้าวหอมมะลิ

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry exists for this customisable restaurant dish in any approved primary source (HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, FAO/INFOODS SEA, or USDA FoodData Central). Nutrient values are estimated as a weighted composite of USDA SR Legacy entries for the most typical bowl configuration. Actual nutrient content will vary substantially depending on customer choices of protein, sauce, and portion size. Confidence is therefore low.
notes: Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entries used for this composite estimate. Trans fat is reported as 0.0 g; this is confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy data for the component ingredients (cooked jasmine rice, mixed vegetables, grilled chicken breast without skin) — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. GI of 56 is an analogous estimate derived from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for cooked jasmine white rice, moderated for vegetable and protein dilution effect; this is not a directly measured GI for this dish. Glycemic load per serving = (56 × 74.0) / 100 = 41.4, based on 400 g serving with 18.5 g carbohydrate per 100 g. Sodium estimate of 210 mg per 100 g reflects a mid-range sauce selection; actual sodium may be higher with soy-based or fish-sauce-heavy dressings. Allergen flags are conditional on customer choices. Halal status is not confirmed as the restaurant's certification status was not verifiable from approved sources. The food_group has been reclassified from 'Vegetables' (input) to 'Mixed' as the dish contains rice, protein, and vegetables in significant proportions. The cuisine tag 'Drinks & Beverages' from the input is not applicable to this dish and has been excluded.

Derived GI of 56 from University of Sydney GI Database jasmine rice entry with documented moderation for vegetable and protein dilution.

Documented trans_fat_g = 0.0 as confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central in notes.

Set confidence to 'low' reflecting composite estimation from non-exact source match.
Excluded inapplicable 'Drinks & Beverages' cuisine tag from input.
Added Thai local_name and romanized_local to reflect Thai origin of 'Khao' concept.
